location.office_time_required: majority of employees encouraged to spend high quality time in-person via regular onsites; most teams meet once or twice per quarter for 2-4 consecutive days at a time

level_signals: 10+ years of product management experience required
Comp range $187,900–$260,000 base, consistent with Principal IC level
Expected to operate cross-functionally across product lines and influence executive stakeholders

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with a strong track record driving consumer engagement, growth, or retention for digital products.
must_haves.qualifier: consumer engagement, growth, or retention for digital products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep fluency in product discovery and hypothesis-driven development, including A/B testing, experimentation frameworks, and success metric definition.
Exceptional collaboration skills, with a proven ability to align diverse stakeholders - from executive leaders to cross-functional partners - around a shared strategy.
A user-first mindset, with the ability to distill complex customer insights into elegant, high-utility product experiences.
Strong judgment and prioritization skills, able to identify high-leverage opportunities and navigate trade-offs with confidence and clarity.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building consumer products for technology companies, preferably in highly regulated industries (financial services, healthcare, etc.)
Proven track record of building and scaling multi-product ecosystems or marketplaces in a B2C environment.
Strong customer-centric mindset with the ability to translate customer insights into actionable product features and enhancements.

scope.surface: User engagement across Upstart's web and mobile consumer credit marketplace; in-product experiences, retention touchpoints, and upsell/cross-sell pathways
scope.business_model: B2C AI lending marketplace partnering with banks and credit unions
primary_metrics_inferred: User retention and repeat usage rates
core_responsibilities: Define and deliver the vision for engagement across Upstart's ecosystem, building experiences across web and mobile that keep users returning and pave the path to upsell and cross-sell.
Champion the user journey, crafting intuitive and rewarding experiences that build trust and increase lifetime value.
Drive experimentation at scale, using rapid iteration and clear metrics to test what works.
Lead with influence, collaborating across product lines and teams to stitch together a cohesive experience across our suite of offerings.
Balance vision and execution, making strategic trade-offs across short-term optimization and long-term differentiation.

compensation.equity_form: Annual equity grants that vest quarterly; Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) with discounted stock purchase options (US only)

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and competitive ($187,900–$260,000 base for US remote)
Fully remote-eligible across the US (and Canada with some restrictions)
Clear product surface and strategic ownership described (engagement / growth pillar)
Strong mission alignment around financial inclusion and reducing cost of borrowing
Generous benefits including 401(k) match up to $15,000, equity, ESPP, wellness allowance
red_flags: Regular in-person onsites required (1-2x per quarter, 2-4 consecutive days) despite remote designation — adds travel burden
Canada-based candidates cannot be hired in Quebec
Canadian base salary range ($182,800–$230,000 USD) — currency basis may be confusing (listed as USD for Canadian role)
10+ years minimum is a hard filter that excludes many strong senior PMs with fewer years
No visa sponsorship information provided — status is unclear

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Principal' + comp range + 10+ years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: Role is listed as 'Remote' but JD body describes regular in-person onsites (1-2x per quarter, 2-4 days); classified as remote-us given the primary designation but travel expectation is notable.
Canadian salary range is listed in USD — unclear if this is intentional or a formatting oversight.

The third preferred qualification ('Strong customer-centric mindset...') largely overlaps with the must-have 'user-first mindset' requirement; treated as a soft preferred given its placement under Preferred Qualifications.
